title: Syracuse’s Boy Mayor: This 14-year-old helmed the city for one day in 1926
summary: Historical article about a 14-year-old who served as Syracuse's mayor for one day in 1926, despite official records crediting Charles Hanna as mayor that year. The piece explores an obscure episode in Syracuse's civic history with potential implications for historical record accuracy and institutional memory.
